prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS/UKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 for the neutral (cation: charge +1 mult 2; anion: charge −1 mult 2). Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization). Partial charges were Mulliken-partitioned. The finite-difference Fukui indices are:

Atom 1 (O):  f+ = 0.46813824, f- = 0.46742464, f0 = 0.46778144, Δf = +0.00071360
Atom 2 (C):  f+ = 0.70839130, f- = 0.11244422, f0 = 0.41041776, Δf = +0.59594709
Atom 3 (H):  f+ = −0.08826939, f- = 0.21007563, f0 = 0.06090312, Δf = −0.29834503
Atom 4 (H):  f+ = −0.08826015, f- = 0.21005551, f0 = 0.06089768, Δf = −0.29831566

Σf+ = 1.0000, Σf- = 1.0000 (charge conservation confirmed). The headline value reported is the largest f+ (most electrophilic site): C atom, f+ = 0.7083913. Most nucleophilic site: O atom, f- = 0.46742464. The large positive dual descriptor on C (+0.5959) confirms it as the primary nucleophilic-attack site, consistent with carbonyl electrophilicity. No warnings were reported; integrity.trustworthy = true.
